title: History: remove Export audit disabled button and Return to review queue
description: Export audit button is always disabled with no explanation - looks broken. Return to review queue as a prominent action from history is wrong affordance - history is a terminal state.
design: Remove Export audit button entirely (or add tooltip: Available in a future release if keeping the slot). Remove the Navigate the flow section containing Return to review queue. Replace with a simple Back to detail breadcrumb link. History should not push users back into a potentially-completed workflow.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Export audit button is removed or has explanatory tooltip
- [ ] Return to review queue is removed from history page
- [ ] History page has a clear Back to detail link
- [ ] No other affordances that incorrectly imply history is non-terminal
close_reason: Implemented in history-page.tsx: removed Export audit and Navigate the flow, trigger-source icons

title: Remove ts-rest, install better-fetch
description: Replace ts-rest dependencies with better-fetch in workspace catalog and package manifests. Remove @ts-rest/core and @ts-rest/react-query from packages/api and apps/web dependencies. Add @better-fetch/fetch via catalog. Ensure bun.lock resolves consistently with single zod major chosen by project.
design: Dependency migration only; no runtime behavior changes in this step.
acceptance_criteria: 1) No @ts-rest dependencies remain in package.json files. 2) @better-fetch/fetch is installed from catalog. 3) bun install succeeds and lockfile is consistent.
close_reason: Done: removed ts-rest deps, added @better-fetch/fetch catalog, refreshed lockfile.

title: Build Portier sync console take-home
description: Deliver the take-home frontend as a coherent operations-oriented sync console covering integrations overview, sync detail, review/conflict resolution, history/audit, live Sync Now API integration, and Dockerized local run support.
design:
Full rewrite sequence:
1. Rewrite shared shadcn primitives to eliminate the unwanted base-lyra sharpness while preserving the Graphite Ops theme.
2. Extract feature-first sync-console domain, API, and session-state modules under apps/web/src/features/sync-console.
3. Keep TanStack Router route files thin: validate params, render feature screens only.
4. Use TanStack Query only for live Sync Now mutation/cache and TanStack Form only for review resolution editing.
5. Implement overview and history on top of shared selectors.
6. Implement detail + review interactive flow on top of query + session state.
7. Add Docker/docs, then code review and verification before push.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Integrations overview, detail, review, and history flows are implemented
- [ ] Sync Now calls the provided API and surfaces loading, success, empty, and failure states honestly
- [ ] Conflict review supports explicit per-field resolution choices
- [ ] Docker build and run path works locally
- [ ] README reflects final architecture, assumptions, and run instructions
notes:
COMPLETED: Replaced stale UX docs with operations-console direction and rewrote Google Stitch prompts.
IN PROGRESS: Setting up detailed execution graph in beads before implementation.
NEXT: Start with route shell, navigation, and shared sync state model.
KEY DECISION: User narrowed scope from interactive take-home flow to static design-first pages so the repo can show UI direction before behavior.
NEXT: Build simple non-interactive screens for overview, detail, review, and history using the updated UX docs.
COMPLETED: Confirmed Graphite Ops theme direction with user and replaced the stock neutral token set.
IN PROGRESS: Moving from design-preview surfaces toward full implementation on top of the new theme.
NEXT: Replace static mockups with real product flows and data/state wiring.
KEY DECISION: Full rewrite now targets the actual product, not static previews.
NEXT: Start with primitive/system rewrite and shared state contracts, then execute the feature issues to closure.
COMPLETED: Web research + oracle consultation refined the architecture.
KEY DECISION: Move to a feature-first slice under apps/web/src/features/sync-console with thin TanStack Router routes, TanStack Query for live Sync Now only, feature-owned session state for simulated workflow, and TanStack Form scoped to review resolution editing.
NEXT: Extract domain/api/state first, then split screens into feature hooks/components.

title: Capture and preserve updated UX direction
description: Record the new operational UX direction, wireframes, and generation prompts so implementation work has a stable source of truth.
design: Keep docs/UI_PATTERNS.md as the UX source of truth, docs/GOOGLE_STITCH_PROMPTS.md as the design-generation source, and align README plus project context references.
acceptance_criteria:
- [x] UI patterns document reflects operations-console UX
- [x] Google Stitch prompt matches the new product direction
- [x] README and project context reference the new docs coherently
notes:
COMPLETED: Rewrote UI_PATTERNS.md, GOOGLE_STITCH_PROMPTS.md, PROJECT_CONTEXT.md section, and README documentation links.
KEY DECISION: The repo will not follow the old minimalist reference literally; it will use a higher-trust operations-console UX.
NEXT: No further work unless implementation uncovers doc drift.
close_reason: Documentation source of truth updated before implementation

title: Rewrite shadcn primitive layer for Graphite Ops
description: Replace the sharp-edged base-lyra feel with a softer but still operational primitive layer so the full product is built on intentional UI foundations.
design: Update the installed shadcn component source in packages/ui/src/components instead of papering over styles at call sites. Remove the hard rounded-none posture, rebalance spacing, improve depth, and make status-bearing components feel trustworthy rather than template-like. Start with button/card/input/badge/alert/tabs/table/checkbox/dropdown-menu/separator, then adjust any app layout classes that still fight the new primitives.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Core primitives no longer read as hard-edged base-lyra
- [ ] Radius, spacing, borders, and hover/focus states feel coherent across primitives
- [ ] Graphite Ops theme tokens and primitive styling reinforce each other
- [ ] No screen depends on one-off local overrides to hide primitive problems
notes:
COMPLETED: Graphite Ops tokens are in place.
IN PROGRESS: Primitive rewrite needs to happen before the full product implementation.
NEXT: Update shared UI components and then re-evaluate app screens under the new primitives.
COMPLETED: Graphite Ops token layer committed.
IN PROGRESS: Rewriting primitives away from sharp base-lyra geometry.
NEXT: Update the shared UI components, then refit app screens to the refined primitive language.
COMPLETED: Core primitives rewritten for softer Graphite Ops language and verified with build + LSP diagnostics.
NEXT: Refit app shell/screens and close the primitive issue once the product screens no longer depend on preview-only composition.
COMPLETED: The rewritten primitive system is now the active UI layer for the interactive product.
NEXT: No further primitive rewrite planned unless review finds inconsistencies.

title: Build shared sync domain model and app state layer
description: Create the typed data model, route-facing selectors, and query/state plumbing that interactive overview, detail, review, and history screens will use.
design: Introduce a domain module in apps/web/src/lib for integrations, histories, review batches, resolution state, and API normalization. Add TanStack Query provider wiring in the router/root so Sync Now can fetch live data. Keep mocked seed data for non-live surfaces, but make the interactive routes consume one shared contract rather than hard-coded page-only mockups.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Shared typed selectors exist for overview, detail, review, and history screens
- [ ] TanStack Query is wired into the app shell
- [ ] API normalization separates success, empty, and failure states cleanly
- [ ] Interactive routes read from one shared contract rather than page-local mock data
notes:
COMPLETED: apps/web/src/lib/api-types.ts provides the initial type vocabulary.
IN PROGRESS: Need a proper domain/state layer before implementing real interactions.
NEXT: Build normalized models and query wiring, then point screens at them.
COMPLETED: Research and oracle consultation confirmed the state boundary: Query for live preview fetch, session store for simulated workflow, Form for resolution editing only.
IN PROGRESS: Extracting domain/api/state from the current monolith into feature-first modules.
NEXT: Create feature slice folders, move pure logic first, then repoint screens.
COMPLETED: Feature-first domain/api/state modules now own sync console workflow state, TanStack Query live fetch, and TanStack Form review resolution editing.
NEXT: No further state extraction planned unless review finds boundary issues.

title: Conduct code review and UX audit before handoff
description: Perform a deliberate self-review of the finished implementation for correctness, maintainability, and UX integrity before final verification and submission.
design: Review the finished diff for consistency with the UX blueprint, theme system, accessibility basics, truthful failure handling, and maintainability. Fix issues before final verification. Use LSP diagnostics and targeted reads instead of eyeballing from memory.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Core flows reviewed for UX consistency and truthful state handling
- [ ] Theme usage is coherent and primitive overrides are justified
- [ ] Accessibility and navigation basics are checked on the main routes
- [ ] Review findings are either fixed or explicitly tracked before final verification
notes: Starting deliberate review on sync-console after API/query cutover and route migration; using reviewer agent + LSP diagnostics + targeted fixes.
close_reason: Completed reviewer audit and fixed findings: syncNow now returns fresh batch for detail success card; provider now reconciles integrations with query updates while preserving active local mutations.

title: Build app shell, route map, and navigation chrome
description: Replace placeholder scaffold screens with the core application shell and route structure required by the sync console.
design: Create a root layout with product header and shared status context, then add route files for overview, integration detail, review, and history. Keep route boundaries aligned with the UX blueprint for future iteration.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Root shell reflects Portier sync console identity
- [ ] Route files exist for overview, detail, review, and history
- [ ] Navigation between views is coherent and preserves user orientation
- [ ] Placeholder scaffold content is removed
notes:
COMPLETED: Placeholder scaffold identified in apps/web/src/routes/index.tsx and root/header files.
IN PROGRESS: Waiting to start implementation after beads graph setup.
NEXT: Replace root shell and add route map.
KEY DECISION: This issue now focuses on static page composition and route structure, not live interactions.
IN PROGRESS: Building shell and page routes for design review.
COMPLETED: Applied Graphite Ops theme tokens in packages/ui/src/styles/globals.css and tuned the root backdrop to match.
KEY DECISION: Theme stays dark, operational, and restrained: graphite surfaces, steel borders, cyan info emphasis, amber warnings, red only for destructive states.
NEXT: Continue from shell/routes into real page implementation under the new theme.
COMPLETED: Initial shell/routes/design foundation committed as milestone feat: establish sync console design foundation.
IN PROGRESS: Blocked on primitive rewrite before final shell polish.
COMPLETED: App shell, route map, and navigation chrome now support overview/detail/review/history flows on the live implementation.
NEXT: No further shell work expected unless review finds navigation defects.

design: Use local modeled integration data to render a dense but readable table plus a top summary strip and priority queue. Include status semantics for synced, syncing, conflict, and error.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Overview shows integration status, pending review count, last sync, and version
- [ ] Search and status filtering work against the local model
- [ ] Priority review queue highlights integrations requiring action

notes:
COMPLETED: Current repo has API types and integration seed data that can back this view.
IN PROGRESS: Not started.
NEXT: Implement after app shell is in place.
COMPLETED: Overview inventory, search, status filtering, and priority queue are implemented in the interactive app.
NEXT: No further overview work unless review finds UX issues.

title: Implement integration detail surface and sync preview entrypoint
description: Build the integration detail page with health context, metrics, preview summary, and the Sync Now trigger.
design: Render reliability signals, summary metrics, and a preview section fed by mocked/local state until Sync Now fetches a live batch. Use clear action hierarchy with Sync Now as the primary trigger.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Detail view shows status, version, metrics, and health banner
- [ ] Sync Now is prominent and correctly disabled/loading when active
- [ ] Preview summary or empty-state communicates what the next review step contains

notes:
COMPLETED: Reference UI inspected; new direction requires more context than the original detail page.
IN PROGRESS: Not started.
NEXT: Implement after shell and overview establish shared layout patterns.
COMPLETED: Detail surface now shows health, metrics, preview summary, and live Sync Now entrypoint.
NEXT: No further detail work unless review finds issues.

title: Implement review queue and field-level conflict resolution
description: Create the high-trust review workflow for safe changes and ambiguous conflicts, including explicit field-level resolution choices.
design: Separate safe updates from conflicts, group changes by entity, show focused local-vs-external comparison, and require explicit resolution before final apply. Model resolution state locally using the API payload as the source for preview content.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Review page distinguishes safe changes from conflicts
- [ ] User can inspect local vs external values for each field
- [ ] User can choose keep local, accept external, or edit merged value where appropriate
- [ ] Final apply control reflects unresolved conflict state truthfully
notes:
COMPLETED: UX blueprint and wireframe for the review flow are documented.
IN PROGRESS: Not started.
NEXT: Build after detail page can hand off a sync batch.
COMPLETED: Review queue supports selection, focused comparison, per-field resolution, and apply flow with TanStack Form-backed resolution editing.
NEXT: No further review work unless review finds defects.

title: Implement sync history and audit timeline
description: Build the history surface for inspecting versions, triggers, results, and changed fields over time.
design: Use mocked/history-modeled data to render a dense, expandable event list or timeline that complements the review flow. Keep version, actor/source, result, and field counts visible in collapsed state.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] History view lists sync events with timestamp, version, source, and result
- [ ] Expanded event state reveals changed-field details or notes
- [ ] History layout feels audit-oriented rather than a plain generic table
- [ ] Navigation back to detail/review is preserved
notes:
COMPLETED: Reference history page inspected; current target is a richer audit-oriented surface.
IN PROGRESS: Not started.
NEXT: Implement after shell patterns are stable.
COMPLETED: History view supports timeline inspection and expanded event detail.
NEXT: No further history work unless review finds issues.

title: Integrate Sync Now API client and state/error mapping
description: Wire the Sync Now button to the provided endpoint and translate live API results into preview, empty, and error states that the UI can trust.
design: Use TanStack Query or equivalent request state handling for the Sync Now action. Normalize success payloads into review state, and map 4xx/500/502 or fetch failures into explicit user-facing states without faking success.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Sync Now calls the provided endpoint for the active integration
- [ ] Successful payloads populate preview/review state
- [ ] Empty/no-change responses are handled explicitly
- [ ] 4xx, 500, 502, timeout, and fetch failures surface truthful user messages
- [ ] No failure path masquerades as applied sync success
notes:
COMPLETED: API endpoint behavior investigated; direct fetches were unstable and must be treated as a first-class UX constraint.
IN PROGRESS: Not started.
NEXT: Implement alongside detail and review pages so live state feeds the right surfaces.
COMPLETED: Sync Now uses TanStack Query against the provided endpoint and maps live results into the review flow.
NEXT: No further API work unless review finds error-state defects.

- [ ] Container can run the app locally with documented port mapping

Docker build attempted twice but blocked by Docker Hub token fetch reset when pulling oven/bun:1.3.9; follow-up issue portier-sync-14k.8.1 created for network-enabled validation.

title: Implement TanStack Form composable for conflict resolution
description: Replace the current review resolution form with TanStack Form composable patterns using createFormHook, withForm, and form.Subscribe for granular reactivity.
design:
Current implementation:
- features/sync-console/review/components/review-resolution-form.tsx (108 lines)
- features/sync-console/review/forms/use-review-resolution-form.ts (45 lines)

Target implementation using TanStack Form v1 patterns:

1. Create form contexts and hook:
```typescript
// forms/resolution-form.ts
export const { fieldContext, formContext, useFieldContext, useFormContext } = 
  createFormHookContexts()

export const { useAppForm, withForm } = createFormHook({
  fieldContext,
  formContext,
  fieldComponents: { ResolutionField, NotesField },
  formComponents: { SubmitButton, FormProgress },
})
```

2. Create composable field components:
- ResolutionField: Per-conflict resolution choice (local/external/merge)
- NotesField: Optional notes textarea
- DiffField: Side-by-side diff display

3. Use withForm for sub-forms:
```typescript
export const ConflictListSection = withForm({
  fieldContext,
  formContext,
  props: { conflicts: Conflict[] },
  render: ({ form, props }) => (
    // Render conflict list with resolution fields
  ),
})
```

4. Use form.Subscribe for reactive UI:
```typescript
<form.Subscribe selector={(state) => [state.canSubmit, state.isSubmitting]}>
  {([canSubmit, isSubmitting]) => (
    <button disabled={!canSubmit || isSubmitting}>
      {isSubmitting ? 'Applying...' : 'Apply Resolution'}
    </button>
  )}
</form.Subscribe>
```

5. Zod validation schema:
```typescript
const resolutionSchema = z.object({
  resolutions: z.record(z.enum(['local', 'external', 'merge'])),
  applyNotes: z.string().optional(),
})
```

Files to create:
- forms/index.ts
- forms/resolution-form.ts
- forms/conflict-list-section.tsx
- forms/fields/resolution-field.tsx
- forms/fields/notes-field.tsx
- forms/fields/index.ts
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] createFormHookContexts() called to create field/form contexts
- [ ] createFormHook() creates useAppForm with field/form components
- [ ] ResolutionField component created for per-conflict resolution choice
- [ ] NotesField component created for optional notes
- [ ] SubmitButton uses form.Subscribe for reactive disabled state
- [ ] FormProgress component shows isDirty/isValid state
- [ ] withForm used to create ConflictListSection sub-component
- [ ] Zod validation schema defined (resolutions record, notes optional)
- [ ] Resolution form validates before submit
- [ ] Form submission calls applyResolution with form data
- [ ] All form components in review/forms/ directory

title: Refactor sync-console into proper feature slices
description: Restructure the sync-console feature into clear slices (overview, detail, history, review) with explicit segments (ui, hooks, lib, types). Move code from components/sync-console/ into feature slices.
design:
Current structure (to be replaced):
components/sync-console/overview-page.tsx (149 lines, mixed concerns)
components/sync-console/review-page.tsx (444 lines, critical refactoring target)
components/sync-console/detail-page.tsx (132 lines)
components/sync-console/history-page.tsx (105 lines)

Target structure:
features/sync-console/
├── index.ts                    # Public API only
├── providers/
│   └── sync-session-provider.tsx
├── domain/                     # Pure business logic
│   ├── integration.ts
│   ├── sync-history.ts
│   └── review.ts
├── shared/
│   ├── ui/                     # PageShell, StatusBadge, etc.
│   └── lib/                    # formatters, selectors
├── overview/
│   ├── page.tsx                # Route container (~30 lines)
│   ├── ui/                     # IntegrationsTable, IntegrationRow
│   └── hooks/                  # useIntegrationsList
├── detail/
│   ├── page.tsx
│   ├── ui/                     # DetailHeader, SyncPreviewCard, SyncNowButton
│   └── hooks/                  # useSyncNow
├── history/
│   ├── page.tsx
│   ├── ui/                     # HistoryTimeline, VersionDetailPanel
│   └── hooks/                  # useSyncHistory
└── review/
    ├── page.tsx
    ├── ui/                     # ConflictList, ConflictInspector, SideBySideDiff
    ├── forms/                  # TanStack Form composable
    ├── hooks/
    ├── lib/                    # inline-diff, resolution-rules
    └── types.ts

Key changes:
1. Move pages from components/ into feature slices
2. Extract inline utilities into lib/
3. Create hooks/ for stateful logic
4. Keep ui/ components presentational
5. Feature public API exposes only pages + provider
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] features/sync-console/overview/ slice created with page.tsx, ui/, hooks/
- [ ] features/sync-console/detail/ slice created with page.tsx, ui/, hooks/
- [ ] features/sync-console/history/ slice created with page.tsx, ui/, hooks/
- [ ] features/sync-console/review/ slice created with page.tsx, ui/, forms/, hooks/, lib/
- [ ] features/sync-console/index.ts exports only pages + provider
- [ ] domain/ folder contains pure business logic (integration.ts, history.ts, review.ts)
- [ ] shared/ui/ contains PageShell, StatusBadge, MetricGrid
- [ ] All pages moved from components/sync-console/ into feature slices
- [ ] Inline utilities extracted to lib/ (inlineDiff, resolution-rules)
- [ ] Routes import from feature entrypoint, not deep paths
- [ ] Type checking passes
- [ ] App renders correctly

title: Overview: remove hardcoded header buttons, context-aware table actions
description: Salesforce detail and Review conflicts buttons are hardcoded in the overview header, always visible. Table action column always shows Open regardless of integration status.
design: Remove hardcoded header action buttons entirely. Table action CTA becomes status-aware: conflict → Review updates (primary), error → View logs (secondary/destructive), syncing → Syncing... (disabled), synced/idle → Manage (outline).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No hardcoded integration-specific buttons in the overview header
- [ ] Table action reads Review updates for conflict status (primary style)
- [ ] Table action reads View logs for error status
- [ ] Table action reads Manage for synced/idle status
- [ ] Syncing rows show disabled state
close_reason: Implemented in overview-page.tsx: removed hardcoded buttons, conditional priority queue, context-aware table actions

title: Restructure sync-console: rename shared→ui, flatten review internals
description: Restructure sync-console: rename shared->ui, flatten review internals. Blocked by portier-sync-state (state layer must be clean before restructuring).

title: Navigation, layout, and mockup text overhaul
description: Fix three categories of problems identified in engineering + design + oracle audit: (1) leftover mockup/preview scaffold text visible to users, (2) three simultaneous navigation surfaces on every integration page due to the integration layout route being a passthrough, (3) missing shadcn components that should replace hand-rolled UI (confirmation modal, breadcrumb). Oracle confirmed the correct pattern: integration.$integrationId.tsx becomes a real layout route owning validation + breadcrumb + tab nav; leaf pages own only their content.
design: Two-level chrome model: __root.tsx owns app shell, integration.$integrationId.tsx owns integration-scoped chrome. Install breadcrumb/alert-dialog/tooltip via shadcn CLI from apps/web (routes to packages/ui automatically). Remove nav rows from leaf pages. Header strips all mockup text.

title: Web API adapters: replace local ts-rest query files with @portier-sync/api imports
description: Replace local sync-console API adapter files that still use @ts-rest/core with thin wrappers over @portier-sync/api better-fetch query layer. Keep export names stable to avoid cross-file churn during transition.
design: Adapter-only step. No state/page logic changes in this bead.
acceptance_criteria: 1) No @ts-rest imports in apps/web/src/features/sync-console/api/*. 2) integrations/history/sync adapter exports remain available for existing callers. 3) adapters delegate to @portier-sync/api queryOptions/client APIs.
notes:
Paused by user request: do not touch web yet; keep focus on API layer.
Deferred until after state bead; only sync-preview adapter currently has active consumer in provider.
close_reason: Replaced sync-console API adapters with @portier-sync/api wrappers; removed @ts-rest dependencies from web adapter layer while preserving legacy exports.

title: Review: replace Apply with Commit N Updates + confirmation modal
description: Apply N decisions immediately commits and navigates to history without confirmation. The word decisions is jargon. The number is selectedCount not resolved count. Applying is irreversible.
design: Rename button to Commit N Updates. On click, show a confirmation modal: You are about to apply N changes (X conflicts resolved, Y safe updates). This cannot be undone. [Cancel] [Commit changes]. Only after confirm does applyReview() fire and navigation happen.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Button reads Commit N Updates (N = selected + resolved count)
- [ ] Clicking opens a confirmation modal with a summary of what will be applied
- [ ] Modal has Cancel and Commit changes buttons
- [ ] applyReview() only fires after Commit changes is clicked
- [ ] Navigation to history happens after confirmation, not immediately
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: Query layer: queryOptions for integrations + history
description: Add features/sync-console/api/integrations.query.ts and history.query.ts with queryOptions. Strip createInitialIntegrations, createInitialHistories, createInitialReviewBatches and all seed constants from domain/ files. Domain files become pure logic only.

title: Remove types.ts barrel — import from schema/ directly
description: types.ts is now a no-op re-export of schema/index.ts. Update all 7 intra-package importers (3 contracts, 3 msw/data files, index.ts) to import from schema/ directly, then delete types.ts.

title: Thin out child route files — remove duplicated notFound validation
description: integration.$integrationId.index.tsx, .review.tsx, and .history.tsx each call getIntegrationById(integrationId) and throw notFound() independently. Once the parent layout route owns this validation, the children doing it again is redundant dead code.
design: Remove the loader/beforeLoad/validation block from all three child route files. Each file becomes: createFileRoute('/integration/$integrationId/...') with a component function that simply renders the page component (DetailPage, ReviewPage, HistoryPage) with the integrationId param. The integrationId is still read via Route.useParams() in each page component as needed.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] integration.$integrationId.index.tsx has no notFound() call
- [ ] integration.$integrationId.review.tsx has no notFound() call
- [ ] integration.$integrationId.history.tsx has no notFound() call
- [ ] All three route files still render their respective page components
- [ ] Navigating to an invalid integration ID still shows a 404 (handled by parent)

title: Review: unify focus and select - remove nested checkbox conflict
description: Clicking a row card focuses it (shows detail right panel), but a nested checkbox selects it for apply. Two different click targets on the same element. Users expect click = select.
design: Click row = focus for detail view AND toggle inclusion. Remove separate Checkbox from conflict items - resolution IS the inclusion decision. Safe items keep a subtle checkbox. Focused item gets a strong left-border highlight. No checkbox inside the clickable button area.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Clicking a row focuses it in the right panel
- [ ] Conflict items have no standalone checkbox - resolution state drives inclusion
- [ ] Safe items have a checkbox for include/exclude
- [ ] Active focus is indicated by left-border highlight not just background tint
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: Track proper SSR/MSW architecture for sync console routes
description: Current fix makes sync-console routes client-rendered (ssr: false) so browser-only MSW-backed data does not blank or error during TanStack Start SSR. Follow up by designing a proper SSR strategy: either real server-safe data loading, selective SSR boundaries that preserve shell truthfully, or an MSW/dev architecture that does not depend on client-only mocks for SSR paths.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Root cause and desired SSR behavior are documented
- [ ] Chosen TanStack Start SSR strategy is implemented or explicitly rejected with rationale
- [ ] Overview route no longer relies on temporary ssr: false solely to avoid server errors
- [ ] Dev and SSR behavior are both verified

title: Add Tooltip to Commit button explaining why it is disabled
description: The Commit N updates button on the review page is disabled when there are unresolved conflicts but shows no explanation. Users cannot tell whether it is disabled because of a bug or because they need to act. Tooltip should explain the condition.
design: Wrap the Commit button in TooltipProvider > Tooltip > TooltipTrigger asChild + TooltipContent. When canApply is false and unresolvedConflicts > 0, show: 'Resolve [N] conflict[s] before committing'. When canApply is false and totalToApply === 0, show: 'Select or resolve at least one change'. When canApply is true, no tooltip needed (or omit TooltipContent). Import from @portier-sync/ui/components/tooltip.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Hovering the disabled Commit button shows a tooltip explaining why
- [ ] Tooltip copy is accurate: references unresolved conflict count when relevant
- [ ] Tooltip does not appear when the button is enabled
- [ ] No TypeScript errors

title: Strip mockup/preview scaffold text from header and root
description: header.tsx shows 'Portier / Design Preview' label, 'Static mockup' Badge, and 'Sync console pages' as the app name — all leftover developer scaffold visible to any user who opens the app. __root.tsx has meta title 'Portier Sync Console Preview'. These make the submission look unfinished.
design: header.tsx: remove the eyebrow div ('Portier / Design Preview'), remove the Badge ('Static mockup'), rename the Link text from 'Sync console pages' to 'Portier', remove the hardcoded nav links to specific integration IDs (Overview/Detail/Review/History pointing to salesforce/hubspot). Replace with: a clean left-side product identity (Portier wordmark + 'Sync Console' label linking to /) and nothing else on the right. __root.tsx: change meta title from 'Portier Sync Console Preview' to 'Portier Sync Console'.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No 'Design Preview' text visible anywhere in the rendered header
- [ ] No 'Static mockup' badge visible in the header
- [ ] App name shows 'Portier' or 'Portier Sync Console', not 'Sync console pages'
- [ ] No hardcoded salesforce/hubspot/detail/review links in global header
- [ ] Browser tab title reads 'Portier Sync Console'
- [ ] Header still renders in __root.tsx above Outlet

title: UX redesign: fix all identified sync console UX problems
description: Systematic fix of every UX problem identified in the engineering + design audit. Covers Review page interaction model, Detail page sync flow, Overview page scannability, and History page affordances.
design: Execute in priority order: Review page first (highest evaluator impact), then Overview, then Detail, then History. All fixes ship together as a coherent redesign, not piecemeal patches.
close_reason: All 12 UX redesign issues completed and verified by tsc + vite build

title: Promote integration layout route to own validation + breadcrumb + tab nav
description: integration.$integrationId.tsx is a passthrough <Outlet /> today. Integration ID validation is duplicated in all three child routes. Tab navigation (IntegrationLinkSet) is duplicated in detail-page, review-page, and history-page. Oracle confirmed: the layout route should validate once, render integration chrome once, and let children render only their unique content.
design: Add beforeLoad to the layout route: use Route.useParams() to read integrationId, call getIntegrationById(integrationId), throw notFound() if not found. In the component: render a two-row chrome block above <Outlet />: (1) Breadcrumb using the new shadcn Breadcrumb component — 'Integrations' (Link to /) / integration.name (current, not linked). (2) Tab nav row using Link with activeProps={{ className: 'bg-secondary text-foreground' }} for active state — tabs: Overview (/integration/$integrationId), Review (/integration/$integrationId/review), History (/integration/$integrationId/history). No manual current prop needed — TanStack Router Link handles active detection. Also render the integration name + StatusBadge as a sub-header between breadcrumb and tabs. Use useSyncConsole() or a direct lookup for integration data.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] integration.$integrationId.tsx has beforeLoad that throws notFound() for invalid IDs
- [ ] Breadcrumb renders: Integrations > [Integration Name]
- [ ] Tab nav renders: Overview | Review | History with active state on the current route
- [ ] Active tab is detected from route match, not a manual current prop
- [ ] Integration name and status badge visible in the layout chrome
- [ ] <Outlet /> renders beneath the chrome
- [ ] Chrome appears on detail, review, and history pages consistently

title: Cleanup: remove .js extensions from schema/ imports + fix stale package.json export
Install shadcn breadcrumb, alert-dialog, tooltip into packages/ui
description: Three shadcn components are needed by the redesign but not yet installed. Missing components are blocking the layout route (breadcrumb), review page modal replacement (alert-dialog), and disabled-button explanations (tooltip).
design: Run from apps/web using bun package manager: bunx --bun shadcn@latest add breadcrumb -y && bunx --bun shadcn@latest add alert-dialog -y && bunx --bun shadcn@latest add tooltip -y. CLI auto-routes to packages/ui/src/components/ based on the ui alias in apps/web/components.json. Verify files appear in packages/ui/src/components/ after each add.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] packages/ui/src/components/breadcrumb.tsx exists
- [ ] packages/ui/src/components/alert-dialog.tsx exists
- [ ] packages/ui/src/components/tooltip.tsx exists
- [ ] All three are importable as @portier-sync/ui/components/breadcrumb etc.
- [ ] No TypeScript errors after install

title: Update README with better-fetch decision
description: Update packages/api/README.md with final architecture: domain schema, contract, better-fetch schema adapter, error handling with throw:true, MSW toggle strategy, and queryOptions conventions.
design: Explain rationale/tradeoffs for take-home reviewers.
acceptance_criteria: README reflects implemented architecture and matches code examples exactly.
close_reason: Done: README rewritten for better-fetch architecture, queryOptions conventions, and MSW/OG toggle.

title: Page cutover: detail/history/review pages remove deleted lib store imports
description: Migrate detail/history/review pages away from deleted lib store hooks, wiring them either to state provider or query layer consistently after state bead lands.
design: Page wiring last to minimize churn while provider contracts are stabilizing.
acceptance_criteria: 1) no imports from lib/sync-console-store or lib/api-types in these pages. 2) pages render with current data contracts. 3) page compile errors resolved.
notes: Oracle-validated sequence: shared/ui type boundary first, then detail/review/history null-safe cutover to IntegrationId and query-backed data.
close_reason: Migrated detail/history/review + shared UI type boundary to @portier-sync/api/useSyncSession; removed lib/api-types and lib/sync-console-store imports; added safe empty states and history query merge.

title: Clean up shared.tsx — remove disabled Settings button from IntegrationLinkSet
description: IntegrationLinkSet in shared.tsx renders a permanently disabled Settings button. This advertises a route that does not exist and confuses users about available navigation. Once IntegrationLinkSet moves to the layout route or is inlined there, this is also an opportunity to clean it up entirely.
design: Remove the <Button variant='ghost' disabled>Settings</Button> from IntegrationLinkSet. If IntegrationLinkSet is still used by the layout route, keep the component but without Settings. If IntegrationLinkSet is fully inlined into the layout route and no longer referenced elsewhere, delete it entirely from shared.tsx and remove any now-unused imports (buttonVariants).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No disabled Settings button visible in any integration page navigation
- [ ] IntegrationLinkSet either no longer exists or exists without a Settings placeholder
- [ ] No unused imports remain in shared.tsx after cleanup
- [ ] No TypeScript errors

title: Delete contract/ folder and ts-rest artifacts
description: Delete obsolete ts-rest artifacts after migration: contract folder (if fully superseded), old client exports, and any stale imports/usages. Preserve only one representation of API contract.
design: Full cutover; no compatibility shims.
acceptance_criteria: 1) No dead ts-rest files/imports remain. 2) packages/api exports are coherent and minimal. 3) typecheck passes for packages/api.
close_reason: Done: removed old ts-rest contract files and stale imports; ./contract export now points to api-contract.

title: Replace review-page confirmation overlay with AlertDialog
description: review-page.tsx currently uses a hand-rolled fixed-inset overlay div as the commit confirmation modal. Now that alert-dialog is installed as a proper shadcn component, the custom overlay should be replaced with AlertDialog for accessibility, focus trapping, and consistent styling.
design: Import AlertDialog, AlertDialogContent, AlertDialogHeader, AlertDialogTitle, AlertDialogDescription, AlertDialogFooter, AlertDialogCancel, AlertDialogAction from @portier-sync/ui/components/alert-dialog. Replace the fixed-inset div block with AlertDialog open={showConfirm} onOpenChange={setShowConfirm}. AlertDialogAction onClick calls handleConfirmApply. AlertDialogCancel closes. Keep the same copy: title 'Commit changes', body summarizing resolved conflicts + safe updates count, 'This cannot be undone'.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Confirmation modal uses AlertDialog, not a hand-rolled fixed div overlay
- [ ] AlertDialog has AlertDialogTitle (accessibility requirement)
- [ ] AlertDialogCancel cancels without applying
- [ ] AlertDialogAction calls handleConfirmApply
- [ ] Focus is trapped inside the dialog when open
- [ ] No custom z-index or backdrop div in review-page.tsx

title: State: remove dead state, useReducer refactor, narrow hooks
description: State cleanup: after portier-sync-state, provider owns only reviewBatches + syncErrors + syncingId. Replace useState with useReducer for those 3 pieces. Remove dead state. Depends on portier-sync-state.

title: Review: show resolution state indicator on each list item
description: Each item in the left panel looks identical whether resolved or not. After picking a resolution, the list item must show a visual indicator so the operator can scan at a glance what is done.
design: Pending conflict: orange warning icon. Resolved (any choice): green checkmark. Safe, included: green checkmark. Safe, excluded: gray empty circle. Indicator sits left of the field label. No checkbox for conflict items.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Each list item shows a state indicator (pending/resolved/excluded)
- [ ] Conflicts show orange warning when unresolved, green checkmark when resolved
- [ ] Safe items show checkmark (included) or muted circle (excluded)
- [ ] Indicator updates immediately when resolution is saved
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: Domain type safety: ReviewResolution discriminated union + typedObjectKeys helper
description: Fix domain-level avoidable casts: (1) ReviewResolution interface → discriminated union with explicit { kind: undefined } variant, update makeSeedItem + buildBatchFromApi + all form consumers. (2) typedObjectKeys<T> helper in lib/typescript-utils.ts, replace Object.keys(...) as ApplicationId[] in domain/review.ts (×4) and domain/history.ts (×2). No runtime behavior change — type fixes only.
close_reason: Superseded: ReviewResolution discriminated union migrates as part of portier-sync-query domain cleanup

title: Overview: conditional priority queue, semantic status badges
description: Priority queue always renders even when all integrations are healthy. Status badges use vague labels: Conflict, Synced, Error.
design: Hide priority queue entirely when no integration has conflict or error status - reward a clean state. Rename badges: Conflict → Needs Review (amber), Synced → Healthy (green), Error → Failed (red), Syncing → Syncing (blue). Badge colors must match urgency semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Priority queue section is absent when all integrations are healthy
- [ ] Priority queue appears when at least one integration is conflict or error
- [ ] Status badges use new labels: Needs Review / Healthy / Failed / Syncing
- [ ] Badge variants match urgency (destructive for Failed, warning-amber for Needs Review)
close_reason: Implemented in overview-page.tsx: removed hardcoded buttons, conditional priority queue, context-aware table actions

title: Create  client with throw:true
description: Create better-fetch client module in packages/api using createFetch with schema and throw:true for TanStack Query compatibility. Export typed error alias and any helper needed for consumers.
design: Client remains transport-focused; no query key logic in this module.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Client uses createFetch with schema. 2) throw:true is enabled by default. 3) Query functions can rely on thrown errors (no error-as-value plumbing required).
close_reason: Done: created better-fetch client with baseURL + throw:true and exported ApiError.

title: Clean up legacy code and update documentation
description: Remove old code after migration, update README with new architecture, and ensure all imports use the new feature public API.
design:
Files to remove after migration:
- apps/web/src/components/sync-console/ (move to feature slices)
- apps/web/src/features/sync-console/*/screens/ (remove re-export shells)
- apps/web/src/lib/sync-console-store.tsx (use feature public API)

Files to update:

- apps/web/src/lib/api-types.ts (move to packages/api/src/types.ts)
- README.md (document new architecture)

README sections to add:
1. Architecture Overview
   - Feature-first structure
   - packages/api with ts-rest
   - MSW for development
   
2. Package Structure
   ```
   packages/
   ├── ui/          # Design system primitives
   ├── api/         # ts-rest contract + MSW
   ├── env/         # Environment config
   ├── config/      # Shared tsconfig
   └── infra/       # Deployment
   ```

3. Feature Structure
   ```
   features/sync-console/
   ├── overview/    # Slice: List view
   ├── detail/      # Slice: Sync Now
   ├── history/     # Slice: Version history
   └── review/      # Slice: Conflict resolution
   ```

4. Development Workflow
   - How to run with MSW
   - How to add new endpoints
   - How to update contracts

5. Design Decisions
   - Why ts-rest over raw fetch
   - Why MSW for mocking
   - Why feature slices
   - Why domain stays in feature
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] apps/web/src/components/sync-console/ removed (moved to features)
- [ ] apps/web/src/features/sync-console/*/screens/ removed
- [ ] apps/web/src/lib/sync-console-store.tsx removed
- [ ] apps/web/src/routes/* updated to import from feature entrypoint

  - [ ] Architecture Overview section
  - [ ] Package Structure diagram
  - [ ] Feature Structure diagram
  - [ ] Development Workflow (MSW usage)
  - [ ] Design Decisions (ts-rest, MSW, feature slices)
- [ ] No orphaned imports or dead code
- [ ] Type checking passes
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] App runs correctly

title: Remove nav rows from detail-page, review-page, history-page
description: Each of detail-page.tsx, review-page.tsx, and history-page.tsx renders a nav row containing a back-link (All integrations / Back to detail) and IntegrationLinkSet (Overview | Review | History tabs + disabled Settings). Once the layout route owns this navigation, rendering it again in each page creates triple navigation surfaces simultaneously.
design: In each of the three page components, delete the <div className='flex flex-wrap items-center gap-2'> block that contains the LinkButton back-link and <IntegrationLinkSet> component. PageShell and its title/description/actions block stays untouched. detail-page.tsx also remove the 'All integrations' LinkButton from that row. The pages should open directly into their PageShell content with no nav row.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] detail-page.tsx has no LinkButton to '/' (All integrations) or IntegrationLinkSet
- [ ] review-page.tsx has no LinkButton to detail or IntegrationLinkSet
- [ ] history-page.tsx has no LinkButton to detail or IntegrationLinkSet
- [ ] Each page still renders PageShell with its title/description/actions
- [ ] No TypeScript errors from removed imports (LinkButton, IntegrationLinkSet may become unused)

title: Migrate API layer from raw fetch to ts-rest
description: Replace the current raw fetch implementation with ts-rest client. Update all API calls to use the typed contract from packages/api.
design:
Current implementation:
- apps/web/src/features/sync-console/api/sync-preview.ts (raw fetch)
- apps/web/src/features/sync-console/api/sync-preview.query.ts (mutation wrapper)

Migration steps:
1. Install ts-rest dependencies:
   - @ts-rest/core
   - @ts-rest/react-query

2. Define contract in packages/api/src/contract/sync.ts:
   ```typescript
   export const syncContract = c.router({
     preview: {
       method: 'GET',
       path: '/api/v1/data/sync',
       query: z.object({ application_id: z.string() }),
       responses: {
         200: SyncPreviewResponseSchema,
         400: ErrorSchema,
         500: ErrorSchema,
         502: ErrorSchema,
       },
     },
   })
   ```

3. Create client in packages/api/src/client/:
   ```typescript
   export const syncClient = initQueryClient(syncContract, {
     baseUrl: 'https://portier-takehometest.onrender.com',
   })
   ```

4. Update feature hooks to use ts-rest client:
   - Replace useMutation with syncClient.preview.useQuery()
   - Type-safe params and response
   - Built-in error typing

5. Remove old api/ files after migration

Benefits:
- End-to-end type safety
- Auto-generated query keys
- Contract-first design
- Zod runtime validation
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] @ts-rest/core and @ts-rest/react-query installed
- [ ] sync-preview.ts (raw fetch) replaced with ts-rest client calls
- [ ] sync-preview.query.ts updated to use ts-rest query client
- [ ] API calls use syncClient.preview.useQuery() or useMutation()
- [ ] Request params are type-checked (application_id must be valid)
- [ ] Response types are inferred from contract
- [ ] Error handling uses typed error responses from contract
- [ ] Old fetch code removed
- [ ] Feature hooks updated to use new client
- [ ] Sync Now functionality works with ts-rest

title: Review: auto-save resolution on choice click, remove Save button
description: There are two submit actions: Save resolution (per-field) and Apply N decisions (final commit). This two-step flow is never explained. Clicking a choice should save immediately.
design: Keep local / Accept external: clicking the button immediately calls updateReviewDecision and advances focus to next unresolved item. Create custom value (merged): still requires Confirm button because there is text input to validate. Remove the standalone Save resolution button entirely.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Clicking Keep Portier Value or Use [Name] Value saves immediately, no extra button
- [ ] Focus auto-advances to next unresolved item after save
- [ ] Create custom value still has a Confirm button
- [ ] Save resolution button is removed from the form
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: Contract: integrations + history endpoints in packages/api
description: Add contract/integrations.ts: GET /api/v1/integrations -> Integration[], GET /api/v1/integrations/:id -> Integration. Add contract/history.ts: GET /api/v1/integrations/:id/history -> SyncHistoryEntry[]. Wire both into contract/index.ts barrel. Depends on types bead.

title: Integrate overview list page with @portier-sync/api query layer
description: Refactor sync-console overview page to read integrations from @portier-sync/api query layer (better-fetch queryOptions) instead of legacy sync store. Keep current UX sections (metrics, priority queue, inventory table, filters).
design: Use domain helpers in features/sync-console/domain/integration.ts for metrics and relative time. Pending-review count has no canonical source in this step; use explicit temporary fallback and TODO for follow-up integration with review batch state.
acceptance_criteria: 1) overview/page.tsx uses useSuspenseQuery(integrationsListQueryOptions). 2) No imports from lib/api-types or lib/sync-console-store in overview page. 3) Existing search/status filter behavior preserved. 4) apps/web typecheck no longer includes the overview string->ApplicationId mismatch error.
close_reason: Done: overview page now sources integrations via useSuspenseQuery(integrationsListQueryOptions), removed legacy lib imports, uses domain helpers for metrics/priority/time, preserved filters/table/actions, and replaced pending-review with explicit TODO fallback (0).

title: Route param validation: params.parse + parseApplicationId guard
description: Use Zod directly in TanStack Router params.parse (official current API — parseParams is deprecated). Step 1: convert ApplicationId from plain TS union to Zod-derived type in lib/api-types.ts — ApplicationIdSchema = z.enum([...]), ApplicationId = z.infer<typeof ApplicationIdSchema>. Step 2: add params: { parse, stringify } to layout route integration.$integrationId.tsx using ApplicationIdSchema.safeParse() + throw notFound() on failure. Step 3: remove all 5 'as ApplicationId' casts in child route components — Route.useParams() now returns inferred ApplicationId. No custom parseApplicationId helper function needed.
close_reason: Superseded: route param is z.string() not z.enum(slugs); validation via query lookup covered by portier-sync-state

title: Review: rename resolution choices to explicit source-aware labels
description: Keep local / Accept external / Edit merged value are ambiguous. External does not tell the user which system. Local does not tell the user which system.
design: Keep local → Keep Portier Value. Accept external → Use [Integration Name] Value (e.g. Use HubSpot Value - dynamic based on integration.name). Edit merged value → Create Custom Value.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Keep local button reads Keep Portier Value
- [ ] Accept external reads Use [Integration Name] Value with the actual integration name injected
- [ ] Edit merged value reads Create Custom Value
- [ ] Labels are consistent everywhere the choices appear
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: State cleanup: strip read data from provider, wire query invalidation
description: Remove integrations + historyByIntegration from sync-session-provider React state. Provider owns only reviewBatches, syncErrors, syncingId. syncNow invalidates integration query after fetch. applyReview invalidates history query. Delete lib/api-types.ts and lib/sync-console-store.tsx after all consumers migrated. Route param becomes z.string() with beforeLoad lookup.
close_reason: Core API and model work complete. Web app migration stashed for separate phase.

title: Route/layout cutover: remove lib/api-types dependency in integration routes
description: Migrate integration route layout and child route loaders from deleted lib/api-types helpers to query-driven lookup using @portier-sync/api integrations query options and notFound handling.
design: Route cutover after adapter/state stabilization.
acceptance_criteria: 1) routes no longer import getIntegrationById/ApplicationId from deleted lib/api-types. 2) route-level integration existence checks use query data. 3) breadcrumb/header continue to show integration name/status.
notes: Ready after 9l4 closure. Remaining legacy imports isolated to integration route files. Next change: remove ApplicationId/getIntegrationById casts, validate integrationId via query-backed data, keep session as mutable UI authority.
close_reason: Removed ApplicationId/getIntegrationById usage from integration routes; routes now pass IntegrationId directly and layout resolves integration from session with API detail query fallback.

title: Replace raw HTML with shadcn primitives (Toggle, Label, Breadcrumb)
description: Install Toggle + ToggleGroup via bunx shadcn (not in packages/ui yet). Replace: (1) overview status filter raw buttons → ToggleGroup + ToggleGroupItem, (2) resolution-choice-field 3-option buttons → ToggleGroup + ToggleGroupItem (shadcn forms rule: option sets 2-7 choices = ToggleGroup), (3) review/history selectable cards → SelectableCard composite (button with aria-selected). ALSO: fix form structure per shadcn Field rules — notes-field raw label → Field + FieldLabel, resolution form divs → FieldGroup + Field + FieldLabel. Breadcrumb in header already implemented. Label component for any remaining raw labels.

title: Input safety: isSyncFetchError type predicate + parseFieldName runtime check
description: Two fixes: (1) isSyncFetchError type predicate in api/sync-preview.ts: replace 'return error as SyncFetchError' in normalizeThrownError with a type predicate that checks all 4 required fields (code, title, message, retryable). (2) Validate field_name format AT THE API CONTRACT BOUNDARY (not in the utility): add z.string().regex(/^\w+\.\w+$/) refinement to field_name in SyncChangeSchema in packages/api/src/types.ts. After this, the split() in parseFieldName receives guaranteed-format input — the as EntityType single boundary cast becomes safe and stays. Do NOT add complex runtime assertions inside parseFieldName itself — validation belongs at the data entry boundary.
close_reason: Superseded: isSyncFetchError + parseFieldName folded into domain cleanup (portier-sync-query + portier-sync-state)

title: History: compact timeline entries with trigger-source icons
description: History timeline entries are verbose. Triggered by operator / Triggered by system is repeated as text on every row, adding noise without scannability.
design: Replace trigger text with compact icons: user trigger → UserIcon, system/scheduled trigger → BotIcon or ClockIcon. Reduce vertical padding on timeline rows. Keep version badge and outcome badge prominent. Move counts (added/updated/deleted) into a compact inline row rather than a 4-column grid.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Trigger source shown as icon not text label
- [ ] Timeline rows are visually compact
- [ ] Change counts (added/updated/deleted) are readable at a glance
- [ ] Selected entry in right panel shows full detail
close_reason: Implemented in history-page.tsx: removed Export audit and Navigate the flow, trigger-source icons

title: State provider cutover: use API query layer and new Integration shape
description: Refactor sync-session-provider/selectors to align with @portier-sync/api Integration type (includes slug) and removal of deleted createInitial* seeds. Provider should no longer import removed domain factories.
design: State remains local for review workflow, but typed to API models.
acceptance_criteria: 1) sync-session-provider.tsx compiles without createInitial* import errors. 2) Integration typing uses @portier-sync/api consistently (id:string, slug:string). 3) selector compile errors from Integration shape mismatch resolved.
notes: Promoted to next action per oracle. Must eliminate legacy lib/api-types in state and deleted createInitial* imports; syncNow must use integration.slug.
close_reason: Cut over provider/selectors to @portier-sync/api types; removed createInitial* factories; syncNow uses integration.slug; added safe guards and hydrated integrations from query options.

title: Phase 2: Web cutover to @portier-sync/api better-fetch query layer
description: Migrate apps/web sync-console from local ts-rest adapters and deleted lib/api-types store to @portier-sync/api exports (queryOptions + keys + schemas).
notes:
Completed slice: portier-sync-hi3 (overview list page integration to @portier-sync/api query layer).
Isolated commit 709d409 landed: overview page migrated to useSuspenseQuery(integrationsListQueryOptions).
Oracle review (agent 39): reorder work to state-first. New order: nbt -> 52w -> 9l4 -> m79. Rationale: current compile blockers are in provider/selectors; adapter-first does not unblock app.

title: Detail: stop auto-navigation on Sync Now, show post-fetch summary
description: Clicking Sync Now immediately navigates to review page. User has no chance to see what was fetched. Auto-redirect removes user autonomy.

- [ ] Sync Now no longer auto-navigates to review

- [ ] Review updates button navigates to review on demand

title: Review: move Approve safe changes to safe-items group header
description: The Approve safe changes button is inside the Resolution controls section (right panel) but operates on all safe items in the left panel. Spatial mismatch. It also implies finality but just bulk-selects.

title: Rewrite queryOptions with better-fetch
description: Rewrite queryOptions modules in packages/api to use better-fetch client and explicit query keys. API shape should accept a single options object that includes an input field plus native TanStack query options passthrough.
design: Keep KISS: concrete per-endpoint queryOptions first; extract helper only after repetition is proven.
acceptance_criteria: 1) integrations/history/sync queryOptions compile and return typed queryOptions. 2) Options shape supports  + TanStack options without manual spread at call sites. 3) queryKey helpers support precise invalidation.
close_reason: Done: queryOptions rewritten to better-fetch with input + TanStack options passthrough and explicit keys.

title: Create API schema with createSchema
description: Introduce a clean contract object in packages/api that references existing domain Zod schemas (schema/*). Contract should define endpoint metadata (path/method/params/query/input/output) without inline schema duplication. Build better-fetch createSchema adapter from this contract as a thin mapping layer.
design: Three layers: domain schema -> contract object -> better-fetch schema adapter.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Contract object exists and reuses existing schemas. 2) createSchema definition is generated from/anchored to contract entries. 3) No duplicated large schema literals in fetch schema file.
close_reason: Done: added clean apiContract object and thin createSchema adapter in fetch-schema.ts.

title: MSW: migrate all seed data into packages/api/src/msw
description: Move ALL seed data from domain/ files into MSW. Add msw/data/integrations.ts: 6 integrations, numeric IDs 1-6 + slugs. Add msw/data/history.ts: history keyed by IntegrationId. Add handlers for new endpoints. Update sync-handler to resolve slug from integration seed. Register all handlers.

title: Refactor: split packages/api/src/types.ts into schema/ folder
description: types.ts was a 140-line monolith. Split into schema/api.ts, schema/sync.ts, schema/integration.ts, schema/history.ts, schema/entities.ts + schema/index.ts barrel. types.ts becomes a one-liner re-export so all external import paths remain unchanged.

title: Fix all avoidable TypeScript 'as' casts + route-param casts
description: SPLIT into uyd-a, uyd-b, uyd-c — see sub-beads

title: Review: monospace font and character-level diff for value panels
description: Local and external values are shown in generic card text. Hard to see exactly what differs at a glance. Source names are generic (Local value / External value) not specific to the integration.
design: Render values in monospace font inside code-block-style containers (bg-muted, rounded). Highlight differing characters with soft red (local) and green (external) text backgrounds. Rename panels from Local value to Portier (current) and from External value to [Integration name] (incoming).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Value panels use monospace font
- [ ] Differing characters are highlighted (red for local-only, green for external-only)
- [ ] Panel titles use specific names: Portier (current) and [Integration] (incoming)
- [ ] Empty values show an explicit em dash, not blank space
close_reason: Implemented in review-page.tsx rewrite: state indicators, auto-save, unified focus/select, Approve all in group header, Commit modal, monospace diff, source-aware labels

title: ts-rest: move client factory to packages/api, fix cross-package type inference
description: ts-rest: client factory covers 3 contracts (sync, integrations, history). Move initClient calls to packages/api, expose typed client per contract. Fix cross-package type inference. Depends on portier-sync-contract.

title: Extract packages/api with ts-rest contract
description: Create a new shared package @portier-sync/api containing ts-rest contract definitions, typed client setup, and MSW handlers for development/testing.
design:
Structure:
packages/api/
├── src/
│   ├── contract/
│   │   ├── index.ts          # Aggregates all contracts
│   │   └── sync.ts           # Sync API contract with Zod schemas
│   ├── client/
│   │   ├── index.ts          # Client initialization
│   │   └── query-client.ts   # TanStack Query integration
│   └── msw/
│       ├── handlers/
│       │   ├── index.ts
│       │   └── sync-handlers.ts
│       ├── data/
│       │   ├── integrations.ts
│       │   ├── history.ts
│       │   └── conflicts.ts
│       └── browser.ts
└── package.json

Contract should define:
- GET /api/v1/data/sync with application_id query param
- Response schemas with Zod (SyncChange, SyncPreviewResponse)
- Error schemas for 400/500/502

MSW handlers should:
- Intercept sync endpoint
- Return mock data from existing domain layer
- Simulate error states for testing
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] packages/api directory created with package.json
- [ ] ts-rest contract defined for sync endpoint (GET /api/v1/data/sync)
- [ ] Zod schemas for SyncChange, SyncPreviewResponse, error types
- [ ] TanStack Query client initialized with initQueryClient
- [ ] Package exports: contract, client, msw handlers
- [ ] Types compile without errors
- [ ] Package can be imported in apps/web

title: Types: Zod schemas in packages/api as single source of truth
description: Add all domain Zod schemas to packages/api/src/types.ts: IntegrationIdSchema (z.string()), SyncStatusSchema (z.enum), IntegrationSchema (id + slug + name + icon + status + lastSynced + version + totalRecords + lastSyncDuration), entity schemas (User/Door/Key + status enums), EntityTypeSchema, SyncSourceSchema, SyncHistoryEntrySchema. Export all from index.ts.

title: Architecture refactor: Feature-first + ts-rest + MSW
description: Refactor the sync-console to proper feature-first architecture with ts-rest contracts, MSW mocking, TanStack Form composable patterns, and extracted packages/api. This demonstrates architectural maturity for the take-home test.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] packages/api extracted with ts-rest contract
- [ ] MSW handlers set up for development mocking
- [ ] Feature slices created (overview, detail, history, review)
- [ ] API layer migrated from raw fetch to ts-rest
- [ ] TanStack Form composable implemented for conflict resolution
- [ ] Clear separation: UI (ui/) / logic (hooks/) / pure functions (lib/)
- [ ] Feature public API exposes only pages + provider
- [ ] All legacy code removed
- [ ] Documentation reflects new architecture
- [ ] Type checking passes
- [ ] Build succeeds
- [ ] Docker runtime works
- [ ] App demonstrates architectural maturity for take-home test
